page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8163 processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 32.0 GB of system memory, which provides a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the ecs.g5ne.2xlarge delivers modest compute performance. Single-core and multi-core CPU tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 25% tier. However, memory bandwidth for large blocks (both read and write) performs in the average range, reaching 38.97 GB/sec and 34.64 GB/sec respectively. Redis SET operations also show average performance at 5.91 million operations per second, while LLM inference speeds for small 135M models are average, though performance drops significantly to the bottom 10% for larger 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g5ne.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density of vCPU to memory without the premium of high-performance modern architectures. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance compared to shared-core alternatives, though the older Intel Xeon architecture limits its raw compute throughput. This profile is best suited for general-purpose workloads that require stable CPU resources and moderate memory, such as basic web serving, Redis caching, lightweight application servers, and small-scale data processing tasks that do not demand high-performance computing or local NVMe storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate modest compute capabilities, with single-core and multi-core scores generally falling into the bottom 25% tier. However, large-block memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations achieve average performance levels. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the ecs.g5ne.2xlarge is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring stable, non-burstable resources. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, including lightweight web serving, caching layers, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for lightweight web serving and caching.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server offering dedicated Intel Xeon cores and stable memory performance for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs at 2.5 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average performance in large-block memory bandwidth and Redis operations
Suited for general-purpose workloads, caching, and lightweight web serving
